reported_metric: DOJ said IcomTech launched in mid-2018 as a purported crypto mining and trading company
market_health_signal: Mining and trading claims required external wallet and venue proof

reported_metric: Victims purchased purported cryptocurrency-related investment products from promoters
market_health_signal: Investor funds entered a platform-controlled return system

reported_metric: Promoters promised guaranteed daily returns from crypto trading and mining
market_health_signal: Fixed daily returns require reconciliation to variable market outcomes

reported_metric: DOJ said withdrawal problems appeared at least as early as August 2018
market_health_signal: Withdrawal friction challenged displayed portal profits

reported_metric: Victims monitored purported returns through online portal accounts
market_health_signal: Dashboard balances needed proof from mining trading and withdrawals

reported_metric: Promoters claimed Icoms would gain value when accepted for goods and services
market_health_signal: Token issuance reframed liquidity stress as future token utility

reported_metric: DOJ said IcomTech stopped making payments to victims by about the end of 2019
market_health_signal: Portal returns failed redemption and liquidity tests

reported_metric: Brend received 10 years and Rodriguez received eight years after wire-fraud conspiracy convictions
market_health_signal: Trial outcome confirmed sham portal and return claims for those defendants

entities: CFTC; David Carmona; Marco Ruiz Ochoa; Juan Tacuri
reported_metric: CFTC announced more than 1 million USD restitution and 4 million USD civil monetary penalties
market_health_signal: Civil orders added restitution penalties and market-access bans
